name: Contra Check
preceding_figure_names: Double Reverse Spin, Fallaway Reverse and Slip Pivot, Hesitation Change, Reverse Pivot, Reverse Turn
following_figure_names: Chasse From Promenade Position, Weave From Promenade Position, Cross Hesitation, Wing, Left Whisk
notes:
Focus: smoothness, rotation, elasticity, lightness, softness, finish the extension.
Preparations and extension details include: bend/lower left knee, shoulder level off, rotate hips to left; do not send stomach muscle forward; connect center into hip joint; CMBP; RF point back first, toe turned in; keep right heel up; weight split; body rotate left while keeping left side up to Man; keep left toe down; flex both knees; get there first, then give (R) hip and ribs to Man; extend R arm; lift glute and center up; imagine stretching R side; upper body rotation and sway; continuous extension to head; elasticity, smoothness, lightness; do not sit or just go down; bounce up; arms belong to partner, never pull back; extend arm to give space; frame: breathe, feel free, continuous movement; push off back foot into PP; stay very left; start articulating head turn; turn may be made between 2-3 to end in PP moving DC; articulate head; prep for next step: lower first and step big.

steps.action: LF fwd in CBMP, pointing almost LOD
comments: If preferred, could be LF fwd with foot flat.
Body turn to L
CBM
Down with knees slightly flexed

steps.action: RF back in CBMP, feet almost BLOD
comments: The step needs to feel more free.
Lady does not bend straight back, there is rotation and head continues to extend go along body line.
Prep: bend/lower left knee, shoulder level off, rotate hips to left.
Do not send stomach muscle forward, i.e. do not stick tummy up to Man at this time.
connect center into hip joint, hip has to be able to move.
CMBP (do not get into Man's space).
RF point back first, RF toe turned in (for better balance).
Keep right heel up (solid, not heel moving down and then up).
Weight split.
Body rotate to left, but keep left side up to Man (though body is turning left and shaping).
Don't break neck.
Keep left toe down on floor, do not lift L toe.
LF is pretty much flat on floor.
Flex both knees, at this time, weight in the middle, not on back foot.
Get there first, then give (R) hip and ribs to Man for extension.
Extend R arm to Man.
Lift glute and center up to Man. (butt cheek up) and give whole R side to Man.
Imagine stretching R side longer to give to Man more, effect is shaping and extending to left.
Upper body has the look of rotating/shaping to left (but don't rotate away from Man, upper body and hip to Man), head "finishes" the extension with breathing.
The whole body is not one straight piece. There is torch in upper body - rotation and sway (do not just bend backward) and at tail end, stretch even more before recovering.
Continuous extension and all the way to head. Upper body, not locked and still, but stay soft and flexible, go and fill the space Man created with his R arm/hand.
Don't "sit", don't just go "down" which feels heavy. Continuous shaping and then bounce up.
Man start recovering after Lady finish extending head.
Arms belong to partner, never pull arms back. extend the arm to give space.
Frame: breathe, need to feel "free". Create elasticity feeling, continuous movement out and come back (no stopping and then rotate. movement is smooth and light.)

steps.action: Transfer weight back to RF | DW
comments: Transfer weight back to RF
Rise e/o 2

steps.action: Transfer weight fwd to LF | Facing DC against LOD
comments: Push off back foot into PP. Time the push off together with Man.
Feet swivel underneath the body. Need to be individually balanced.
Make sure to stay very left (has tendency to shift to R, get into Man's space). Keep big frame and give R side to Man, don't fall to PP.
At end, start articulating head turn

steps.action: LF to side in PP | FDW, moving along LOD
comments: Turn may be made between 2-3 to end in PP moving DC.

steps.action: RF to side in PP | FDC, moving LOD
comments: Articulate head.
Make sure R side is to Man.
Prep for next step: Lower first and step big.
